sourceMovie.title: Demolition
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Demolition (2016) stuck because it commits to its comedy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jean-Marc Vallée's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance)
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Demolition clicked for you, Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance) (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Demolition?
faq.answer: Try Birdman or (The Unexpected Virtue of Ignorance), A Man Called Otto, Garden State—each shares DNA with Demolition while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Demolition?
faq.answer: Demolition (2016) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Demolition worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
